output.code:
path="/mnt/data/file-L7FP68VDncM1VuqmWxdtnv-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
    s=f.read()
len(s), s[:500]

import re
text=s
aya = "وَمَن يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُ مَخْرَجًا * وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ"
hadith = "يا ابن آدم، تفرغ لعبادتي أملأ صدرك غنى وأسد فقرك، وإن لا تفعل ملأت يديك شغلاً ولم أسد فقرك"
# but hadith starts with "يا ابن آدم،" after "إن الله تعالى يقول:" Now we search occurrences of hadith text maybe with leading "إن الله تعالى يقول:". We'll include only the matn after colon. So use hadith above.
positions_aya=[m.start() for m in re.finditer(re.escape(aya), text)]
positions_had=[m.start() for m in re.finditer(re.escape(hadith), text)]
len(positions_aya), len(positions_had), positions_aya[:5], positions_had[:5]

def make_spans(positions, text, span_text):
    spans=[]
    for st in positions:
        ed=st+len(span_text)
        span=text[st:ed]
        # no surrounding quotes because we extracted exact
        # but check equality
        if span!=span_text:
            print("mismatch", st, span)
        spans.append((st, ed, span_text))
    return spans
aya_spans=make_spans(positions_aya, text, aya)
had_spans=make_spans(positions_had, text, hadith)
len(aya_spans), len(had_spans)

all_spans = []
for st in positions_aya:
    all_spans.append({"span_start":st, "span_end": st+len(aya), "span_text": aya, "citation_type":"Aya"})
for st in positions_had:
    all_spans.append({"span_start":st, "span_end": st+len(hadith), "span_text": hadith, "citation_type":"Hadith"})
all_spans_sorted = sorted(all_spans, key=lambda x:x["span_start"])
len(all_spans_sorted), all_spans_sorted[:3]

for item in all_spans_sorted:
    print(item['span_start'], item['span_end'], item['citation_type'])
